title: lathyrus occidentalis f. lanceolatus
intro: Lathyrus occidentalis f. lanceolatus, also known as Western sweetpea, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the pea family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and expectorant properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as relieving respiratory issues, soothing digestive problems, and supporting cardiovascular health. Western sweetpea can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
